scope: Audit of tool_paper.tex files in halley-labs
note: This file contains confirmed fabrication-level findings, material misrepresentations, and clean completions from the subrepo sweep. The top-level halley-labs/tool_paper.tex is a standalone root-level paper rather than a subrepo paper and is not classified here.

result_issues: Paper text explicitly states that benchmark placeholders were replaced by benchmarks/fix_paper.py with hardcoded values rather than actual experiments.
11+ result tables contain empty or dash-filled placeholders.
Simulated scalability data is presented as empirical evaluation.
Paper makes unsupported production-system claims.
Temporal interaction and filtering-accuracy tables are empty.
Scalability plot uses hardcoded zero-time values.
Negative clinical validation outcomes are omitted from the paper narrative.
Case-study claims are incomplete relative to available result files.

confirmed_fabrications.verdict: simulated and hardcoded benchmark claims presented too strongly
result_issues: Abstract markets LightDP and CheckDP baseline scores as factual without clear abstract-level disclosure that they are simulated.
Perfect F1 benchmark results are driven by hardcoded bug catalogs rather than actual verification.
Real-world mechanism classifications rely on heuristic pattern matching, not formal verification, while being presented as stronger evidence than warranted.
A repository script explicitly describes replacing fabricated empirical numbers in the paper.
Real-world trading claims in the abstract are unsupported by corresponding data artifacts.
An independent verification table appears fabricated, with implausibly identical values across trials.
Sensitivity results include inline comments admitting placeholder values.
Paper contains dozens of visible redaction markers where quantitative values should appear.
Multiple figures use placeholder zero-valued coordinate series while the surrounding text describes substantive empirical trends.
Body tables and narrative make empirical claims that cannot be traced to a credible benchmark derivation trail.
citation_issues: Bibliography resolves formally, but some external corroboration claims are unverifiable from the paper's evidence trail.

result_issues: Abstract and conclusion claim 100% detection and 0% false positives, but available result files show materially different values.
Table-level detection metrics do not appear to be derivable from on-disk benchmark artifacts.
Paper falsely claims a benchmark script is truncated when the repository contains a complete script.
The experiment driver explicitly describes verification times and iteration counts as simulated.
The paper presents those simulated results as empirical benchmark outcomes without adequate disclosure.
Headline speedup and scalability claims are contradicted by the available result files.
Central compression-ratio and state-count claims for 2PC benchmarks are contradicted by the repository's comprehensive result files.
Reported real-world compression reduction claims conflict with the available benchmark JSON.
Paper explicitly states that headline F1 improvements come from an unverifiable static results file and cannot be reproduced.
Evaluation is circular because anomalies are injected and scored against self-generated labels.
Synthetic data is described with real-world framing, and scale claims are materially overstated.

confirmed_fabrications.verdict: fabricated scaling table and placeholder remnants
result_issues: A published scaling table is contradicted by the repository's own benchmark JSON.
The paper still contains unfilled placeholder text in the surrounding performance discussion.
Several other tables show suspiciously uniform values that warrant deeper manual checking.
Reported proof sizes are generated by formulaic heuristics rather than actual solver-produced certificates.
Paper claims Z3/CVC5-backed SMT certification, but the project does not contain the advertised solver integration.
Some case-study and multi-market result claims are presented without implementation-backed evidence.

confirmed_fabrications.verdict: multiple confirmed fabricated tables and metrics
result_issues: Initialization results show identical values across repeated seeds where stochastic variation should exist.
Phase-classification accuracy and configuration counts in the paper do not match the underlying JSON artifacts.
A comparison table reports methods and metrics not present in the implementation or result files.
Loss-reduction claims are not backed by the available experiment outputs.
citation_issues: Some compared methods discussed in the paper are not actually implemented or evidenced by the repository artifacts.

result_issues: Several result tables are present with headers but no data rows.
The paper contains an unsupported comparison against a tool named SpecFuzzer with no citation trail.
Multiple evaluation sections are truncated or incomplete while still being presented as part of the results narrative.
Quantitative sections rely on simulated projections rather than completed measured evaluation.
citation_issues: SpecFuzzer is discussed as a comparator without any supporting citation.

result_issues: Paper claims 20 real-world configurations across four application domains, but repository artifacts only support synthetic benchmark families.
Headline success-rate and win-rate numbers in the current paper are not derivable from the available results files.
The current domain-by-domain results table appears hardcoded relative to the underlying experiment artifacts.

confirmed_fabrications.verdict: systematic fabricated metrics and citation failures
result_issues: Core hypervolume, success-rate, and timing claims are contradicted by the available benchmark data.
Lead contribution claims about infeasibility detection lack supporting benchmark evidence.
Scaling claims are reported far beyond the scale supported by repository experiments.
citation_issues: A large fraction of cited keys are reportedly unresolved from the accompanying bibliography.

confirmed_fabrications.verdict: fabricated SOTA speedup claims mixed with valid sub-results
result_issues: Headline brute-force speedup claims rely on simulated baseline timing rather than actual re-execution.
A larger compound-improvement claim inherits that fabricated baseline comparison.
Published SOTA benchmark table values do not match the raw benchmark JSON available in the repository.

confirmed_fabrications.verdict: severe fabricated speedup and verification claims
result_issues: Ablation speedup values appear unsourced and the benchmark code hardcaps results to match the paper's headline claim.
The paper claims perfect retrieval-quality preservation while later text and benchmark artifacts show measurable degradation.
Verification success is presented as 100% despite repository artifacts recording failures.
citation_issues: Some bibliography entries appear unused, though the core problem is results integrity rather than citation fabrication.

confirmed_fabrications.verdict: simulated SOTA benchmark results presented too empirically
result_issues: The paper's SOTA benchmark results are generated from simulation code rather than actual cryptographic or baseline tool execution.
The paper selectively frames low-accuracy and unacceptable-overhead results as more competitive than the underlying per-scenario data supports.
The paper presents a 16-DAG evaluation table, but repository benchmark artifacts only support a much smaller set of DAGs.
Even for DAGs that do exist in the result file, some fragile-edge counts reported in the paper do not match the available benchmark JSON.
The paper itself repeatedly admits the empirical pipeline is incomplete or preliminary.

confirmed_fabrications.verdict: fabricated empirical comparison and ablation content
result_issues: Comparator columns for Herbgrind and Herbie are manually curated rather than measured by automated runs.
The ablation study includes mathematically impossible counts and is admitted to be approximate rather than executed.
Error magnitudes and headline precision-improvement claims are hand-selected or otherwise not empirically derived from tool execution.
citation_issues: The paper cites Higham where it appears to mean Numerical Recipes, which is a source mismatch.

result_issues: The paper's 15-scenario SOTA benchmark section relies on placeholder-derived coverage and baseline metrics.
The headline coverage and speedup claims are contradicted by the project groundings, which identify hardcoded placeholder values in the solver and baselines.
The paper simultaneously states that full large-scale polytope bounds have not yet been computed while presenting those benchmark results as finished.

confirmed_fabrications.verdict: simulated extended benchmark values presented as results
result_issues: The extended benchmark table for BearSSL and Signal Protocol is backed by simulated or hardcoded values rather than a committed analysis artifact.
The paper presents those entries as a concrete benchmark table even though the grounding file says they require validation on actual binaries.
Some verification-speedup and concurrent-channel numbers also rely partly on modeled or literature-derived values rather than fully reproducible on-disk measurements.
No fabricated metrics were found, but benchmark-family aggregation obscures scenarios where CPA outperforms the cited baseline.
The paper includes a factual error claiming F1 is 0.000 across all scenarios when the underlying results do not support that.

material_misrepresentations.verdict: metric inflation and undisclosed evidence gaps
result_issues: The paper claims 1404% higher combined performance, but that figure appears to misstate a 14.04x ratio as a percentage increase.
Eight evaluation figures were removed because they contained placeholder zero-data, but the abstract does not disclose the missing empirical support.
Key headline improvements are model-predicted rather than human-validated, without adequate abstract-level disclosure.

material_misrepresentations.verdict: disclosed simulation artifacts plus unsupported empirical claims
result_issues: The repository contains a benchmark generator that fabricates solver-performance data, although the paper discloses that the script is only a simulation.
The paper makes an unsupported empirical cache-hit-rate claim.
The abstract and framing overstate the significance of results obtained on many trivial instances.

material_misrepresentations.verdict: uncompiled formalization and simulated baselines overstated
result_issues: The abstract presents an uncompiled Lean formalization as though it established soundness end-to-end.
Tabled baseline comparisons are simulated rather than true external-tool runs, and that distinction is not prominent enough.

material_misrepresentations.verdict: headline throughput remains unverifiable from committed artifacts
result_issues: The paper's main Rust/Criterion throughput claims are not backed by committed benchmark outputs in the repository.
A separate committed Python benchmark suite produces materially different rankings, and the paper only later explains the discrepancy.
The result is a headline performance story that is not independently reproducible from the checked-in artifacts.

clean_completed_audits.verdict: no confirmed fabrications found in the current paper
no confirmed fabrications found in the revised current paper
no confirmed fabrications found in the revised current paper
no confirmed fabrications found; simulation-based comparisons are explicitly disclosed
